title: nuphar japonica var. rubrotincta
intro: Nuphar japonica var. rubrotincta, commonly known as the Japanese water lily or red-stemmed water lily, is a variety of water lily plant native to East Asia. This aquatic plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with its roots and rhizomes exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The medicinal properties of Nuphar japonica var. rubrotincta are believed to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the roots and rhizomes of the plant are often dried and used to make decoctions, infusions, or tinctures that can be consumed as a tea or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
